The **"FlagOS Open Computing Global Challenge"** is a prestigious multi-season competition designed to foster innovation and development in the field of AI. Hosted by the FlagOS Community, Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence (BAAI), and CCF Open Source Development Technology Committee (ODTC), the competition encourages developers to engage in hands-on creation and innovative exploration based on the FlagOS unified AI system software stack.

### Background
The competition aims to enhance the skills of AI developers and promote the thriving development of the open computing ecosystem. With a substantial prize pool of 2 million RMB, winners will not only share the rewards but also receive official honorary certificates from the organizers.

### Main Content
**Season 1** of the competition, which opens for registration on January 9, 2026, is set to conclude with results announced in early June 2026. This season focuses on three core tracks:

1. **Operator Development and Optimization**: This track evaluates participants' capabilities in low-level operator implementation and cross-hardware deep optimization.
2. **LLM Inference Optimization**: This track assesses participants' ability to optimize large model inference performance while maintaining model accuracy and minimizing latency.
3. **Automatic Data Annotation**: This track challenges participants to design effective In-Context Learning (ICL) solutions for automatic data annotation in long-context scenarios.

Each track is designed to target specific dimensions of development expertise, ensuring a comprehensive assessment of participants' skills.

## 📅 Competition Schedule

- **Registration**: January 9 – May 20, 2026
- **Computing Resource Application**: January 9 – March 11, 2026
- **Computing Resource Review & Allocation**: March 12 – March 23, 2026
- **Development**: February 24 – May 20, 2026
- **Submission of Technical Reports and Source Code**: May 21 – May 31, 2026
- **Evaluation**: May 21 – June 4, 2026
- **Results Announcement**: Early June 2026

## 💰 Prize Structure

The prize structure is as follows:

- **Track 1 (Operator Development and Performance Optimization)**: 
  - Easy Operator Task: 1000 RMB per task
  - Medium Operator Task: 2000 RMB per task
  - Hard Operator Task: 3000 RMB per task
- **Track 2 (Large Model Inference Throughput Optimization)**:
  - 1st Prize: 30,000 RMB
  - 2nd Prize: 20,000 RMB
  - 3rd Prize: 10,000 RMB
- **Track 3 (Automatic Data Annotation)**:
  - 1st Prize: 30,000 RMB
  - 2nd Prize: 20,000 RMB
  - 3rd Prize: 10,000 RMB

## 📝 Requirements

### Eligibility and Participation
- All individuals or teams interested in the competition are welcome to register.
- Participants may compete individually or as a team, with a maximum of 3 members per team.
- Each individual or team may only hold one participant identity throughout the competition period.

### Track Selection Rules
- Each participant (individual or team) may only submit work to one track.
- Once a submission is made to any track, the track selection is considered confirmed and cannot be changed.

### Submission Requirements
- All submissions must comply with universally recognized international standards and public order and good morals.
- Content involving security threats, pornography, ethnic discrimination, religious discrimination, or privacy violations is strictly prohibited.
- All submissions must be original work created by the participant (individual or team) with complete intellectual property rights.

## 📝 Important Details

- The competition is open to AI algorithm and system engineers, hardware and compiler developers, and all technical practitioners and visionaries dedicated to overcoming the complexities of AI chip programming.
- Detailed award categories and distribution methods will be announced in subsequent notices.
- By submitting work, participants agree that the organizers and their authorized entities have the right to use the work for promotion, exhibition, publication, and other purposes without compensation.

Competition Overview</h2>
<p class="mb-4 leading-relaxed whitespace-pre-line">The <strong>"FlagOS Open Computing Global Challenge"</strong> is a prestigious multi-season competition designed to foster innovation and development in the field of AI. Hosted by the FlagOS Community, Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence (BAAI), and CCF Open Source Development Technology Committee (ODTC), the competition encourages developers to engage in hands-on creation and innovative exploration based on the FlagOS unified AI system software stack.</p>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Background</h3>
<p class="mb-4 leading-relaxed whitespace-pre-line">The competition aims to enhance the skills of AI developers and promote the thriving development of the open computing ecosystem. With a substantial prize pool of 2 million RMB, winners will not only share the rewards but also receive official honorary certificates from the organizers.</p>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Main Content</h3>
<p class="mb-4 leading-relaxed whitespace-pre-line"><strong>Season 1</strong> of the competition, which opens for registration on January 9, 2026, is set to conclude with results announced in early June 2026. This season focuses on three core tracks:</p>
<ol class="list-decimal list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Operator Development and Optimization</strong>: This track evaluates participants' capabilities in low-level operator implementation and cross-hardware deep optimization.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>LLM Inference Optimization</strong>: This track assesses participants' ability to optimize large model inference performance while maintaining model accuracy and minimizing latency.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Automatic Data Annotation</strong>: This track challenges participants to design effective In-Context Learning (ICL) solutions for automatic data annotation in long-context scenarios.</li>
</ol>
<p class="mb-4 leading-relaxed whitespace-pre-line">Each track is designed to target specific dimensions of development expertise, ensuring a comprehensive assessment of participants' skills.</p>
<h2 class="text-2xl font-semibold mb-3">📅 Competition Schedule</h2>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Registration</strong>: January 9 – May 20, 2026</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Computing Resource Application</strong>: January 9 – March 11, 2026</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Computing Resource Review & Allocation</strong>: March 12 – March 23, 2026</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Development</strong>: February 24 – May 20, 2026</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Submission of Technical Reports and Source Code</strong>: May 21 – May 31, 2026</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Evaluation</strong>: May 21 – June 4, 2026</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Results Announcement</strong>: Early June 2026</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="text-2xl font-semibold mb-3">💰 Prize Structure</h2>
<p class="mb-4 leading-relaxed whitespace-pre-line">The prize structure is as follows:</p>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Track 1 (Operator Development and Performance Optimization)</strong>:
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">Easy Operator Task: 1000 RMB per task</li>
<li class="mb-1">Medium Operator Task: 2000 RMB per task</li>
<li class="mb-1">Hard Operator Task: 3000 RMB per task</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Track 2 (Large Model Inference Throughput Optimization)</strong>:
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">1st Prize: 30,000 RMB</li>
<li class="mb-1">2nd Prize: 20,000 RMB</li>
<li class="mb-1">3rd Prize: 10,000 RMB</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Track 3 (Automatic Data Annotation)</strong>:
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">1st Prize: 30,000 RMB</li>
<li class="mb-1">2nd Prize: 20,000 RMB</li>
<li class="mb-1">3rd Prize: 10,000 RMB</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="text-2xl font-semibold mb-3">📝 Requirements</h2>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Eligibility and Participation</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">All individuals or teams interested in the competition are welcome to register.</li>
<li class="mb-1">Participants may compete individually or as a team, with a maximum of 3 members per team.</li>
<li class="mb-1">Each individual or team may only hold one participant identity throughout the competition period.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Track Selection Rules</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">Each participant (individual or team) may only submit work to one track.</li>
<li class="mb-1">Once a submission is made to any track, the track selection is considered confirmed and cannot be changed.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Submission Requirements</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">All submissions must comply with universally recognized international standards and public order and good morals.</li>
<li class="mb-1">Content involving security threats, pornography, ethnic discrimination, religious discrimination, or privacy violations is strictly prohibited.</li>
<li class="mb-1">All submissions must be original work created by the participant (individual or team) with complete intellectual property rights.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="text-2xl font-semibold mb-3">📝 Important Details</h2>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">The competition is open to AI algorithm and system engineers, hardware and compiler developers, and all technical practitioners and visionaries dedicated to overcoming the complexities of AI chip programming.</li>
<li class="mb-1">Detailed award categories and distribution methods will be announced in subsequent notices.</li>
<li class="mb-1">By submitting work, participants agree that the organizers and their authorized entities have the right to use the work for promotion, exhibition, publication, and other purposes without compensation.</li>
<li class="mb-1">The organizers hold final interpretation rights over all competition rules and arrangements.</li>
</ul></div></div><div class="mt-6 p-4 bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/20 border border-blue-200 dark:border-blue-800 rounded-lg"><p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">This overview is a content summary.
